lenses.guidance: The seventh plague (hail, vv 13-26) introduces a new category: 'Yahweh sent thunder and hail' (v 23). The redemptive arc deploys cosmic-scale weather; covenant Lord commands the elements that Egyptian gods claim.

'That my name may be proclaimed in all the earth' (v 16) is named as the plagues' purpose. Paul cites this at Rom 9:17. The plague cycle is missional theology in narrative — every nation hearing of Yahweh.

v 16's 'raised you up that my name may be proclaimed' is echoed at Rom 9:17 — Paul's argument about election uses Pharaoh as the canonical example. Exodus's local conflict becomes the New Testament's doctrine of sovereign mercy.

Egyptian officials begin to fear Yahweh (vv 20-21) — outsiders hear before Pharaoh hears. Trust that God's word reaches whom it will, and that today the same speech still divides those who shelter from those who don't.
